_description: Dicebound species profile data. This file stores light roleplay profile influences for the official 2024 Player's Handbook core species used by Dicebound. These values can gently bias personality scoring, playstyle wording and weakness text after the question flow has been answered. This file should not alter DnD ability scores, class statistics, saving throws, hit points or mechanical rules.

_sourceNotes.importantNote: These influences are intentionally light roleplay nudges, not fixed personality rules. The character's question answers should remain the main source of personality, alignment, weakness and playstyle.

Aasimar.weaknessBias: Burden. Aasimar may feel pressured to live up to a divine purpose, even when they are exhausted, afraid or uncertain.

Dragonborn.weaknessBias: Pride. Dragonborn may struggle to retreat, apologise or accept being seen as weak.

Dwarf.weaknessBias: Stubbornness. Dwarves may hold to a promise, grudge, craft, clan duty or tradition even when changing course would be wiser.

Elf.weaknessBias: Detachment. Elves may observe for too long before acting, especially when short-lived urgency feels distant to them.

Gnome.weaknessBias: Obsession. Gnomes may chase an idea, mystery, illusion or invention long after caution says to stop.

Goliath.weaknessBias: Competitiveness. Goliaths may turn danger into a test of worth, even when caution or teamwork would save them.

Halfling.weaknessBias: Overcommitment. Halflings may put themselves in danger because they cannot ignore someone who needs help.

Human.weaknessBias: Restlessness. Humans may push too hard because they feel time, opportunity or legacy slipping away.

Orc.weaknessBias: Impatience. Orcs may act quickly because waiting can feel like weakness, hesitation or wasted survival time.

Tiefling.weaknessBias: Defensiveness. Tieflings may expect rejection before it happens and push away people who might have stood beside them.
